title: [Feature Request] Option for automatic text centering on both X/Y axis
author: Krezzin
body: There should be a button to center text on the screen automatically, as of right now you have to manually play with the values to be able to achieve that, both horizontally and vertically. Not really convenient,

title: [Bug] Artifacts appearing when exporting with Gifski
author: 123tontito
body: **Describe the bug** I'm trying to record GIFs for the Deltarune Wiki and the background of the battles always exports with lossy artifacts, I don't know what's the problem. I'm using Gifski with 100% high quality. The dll isn't the problem since somebody else already checked with another computer The version doesn't seem to be the problem because I installed an old one and it still didn't work. The screen recording isn't the problem because when I check the footage recorded it looks perfectly fine. I have a lot of memory in my computer so that doesn't seem to be the problem. The problem always happens when exporting and I seriously don't know what's causing it. **To Reproduce** Steps to reproduce the behavior: 1. Go to 'Save as' 2. Click on Gifski as encoder using the best quality 3. Scroll down to 'Save' 4. Export the file and see problem **Expected behavior** I expected the exported gif to match the preview without being compressed **Screenshots** **Desktop (please complete the following information):** - OS: Windows 11 - Version: 2.43.1

title: Docker image of Screentogif?
author: JN8086
body:
I love screentogif and use it all the time. But all the files I make gifs of are on my server. It would be handy if I could run screentogif on the same server I use to store everything. A docker image of screentogif would solve that problem.

Is a docker image in the works?

title: [Feature Request] Preserve PNG alpha channel without filling solid background when importing PNG images
author: Sarhigat
body: When importing PNG images, transparent areas are filled with a solid color and the original alpha channel is discarded. Please add support to retain the original alpha channel instead of background filling, or provide a toggle to disable background filling during PNG import.

title: [Feature Request] Preview features
author: TristisOris
body:
The program itself is good and convenient. But to make a .gif, I need to know the final size or reach the target size.
- Would like to see the size of the current project immediately, not just after export.
- Sometimes it's impossible to achieve the desired size just by removing extra frames; in that case, I'm ready to lower the image quality - but that will also only be visible after export. Would like to see a preview. How changing the palette or other modes affects it.

title: The ScreenToGif program cannot download the ffmpeg.exe and gifski.dll files it needs.

Tested on ScreenToGif.2.43.1.Light.Portable.x64 and ScreenToGif.2.43.1.Light.Portable.x86

Is it possible to get direct links to these files (ffmpeg.exe and gifski.dll) compatible with Windows 7 x32 and x64

title: [Feature Request] Advanced Capture Tools: Magnetic Rulers & Customizable Mouse Pointer Zoom
author: 0H4S
body:
## 1. Overview
This feature request proposes two major enhancements to ScreenToGif aimed at improving capture precision and enhancing the quality of instructional/tutorial recordings:
1. **Magnetic Rulers & Snapping Guides:** A system to create, move, and snap the recording frame to custom or system-defined screen guides.
2. **Customizable Mouse Pointer Zoom & Effects:** A configurable shortcut-driven zoom effect centered on the mouse cursor, along with the ability to customize or change the cursor style during recordings.

---

## 2. Feature 1: Magnetic Rulers & Snapping Guides

### 2.1. Description
A mechanism allowing users to deploy horizontal and vertical reference lines across the monitor. These lines possess magnetic properties (*snapping*), facilitating millimeter-precise alignment of the ScreenToGif capture window.

### 2.2. Core Functionalities
* **Dynamic Free Guides:** Users can drag horizontal or vertical guides from the screen edges and reposition them freely across any connected monitor.
* **Magnetic Snapping:** When the border of the ScreenToGif recording window approaches a guide line within a configurable threshold (e.g., $5\text{px}$ to $10\text{px}$), it automatically snaps to the line.
* **Persistent Presets:** Ability to save custom ruler layouts as configurations/templates. Users can manage, name, and quickly reload these layouts in future recording sessions to maintain visual consistency.
* **System Smart Guides (Automated Calculations):** The tool dynamically reads the active monitor's resolution and offers automated layout grids, such as:
  * **Center Locator:** Automatically places crosshairs at the exact horizontal and vertical centers of the screen.
  * **Screen Slicing:** Automatically divides the screen into proportional grids or fractions (e.g., $2 \times 2$, $3 \times 3$, or fixed ratios like 16:9).

### 2.3. UI/UX Requirements
* Semi-transparent, customizable color lines to ensure contrast against any desktop background.
* Toggle shortcut to instantly hide/show all rulers.
* A "Lock Guides" option to prevent accidental movement while adjusting the capture area.

---

## 3. Feature 2: Customizable Mouse Pointer Zoom & Custom Cursors

### 3.1. Description
A dedicated shortcut/hotkey function tailored for content creators and educators that creates a localized zoom effect focused directly on the mouse pointer. Additionally, this feature introduces options to override the default system cursor during the recording process.

### 3.2. Core Functionalities
* **Triggered Pointer Zoom:** A highly visible, localized magnification lens that follows the mouse cursor when a hotkey is held or toggled.
* **Granular Zoom Configuration:**
  * **Magnification Factor:** Adjustable scale (e.g., $1.5\times$, $2.0\times$, $3.0\times$).
  * **Radius/Size:** Fully adjustable area of effect (diameter of the zoom lens).
  * **Animation & Smoothness:** Configurable interpolation and easing functions to smooth out the zoom-in/zoom-out transition, preventing jarring visual cuts for the viewer.
  * **Lens Aesthetics:** Options to customize the border width, color, and drop shadow of the magnification lens.
* **Custom Cursor Selection:**
  * Ability to swap the default system mouse pointer with alternative styles specifically during ScreenToGif recording sessions (e.g., high-visibility pointers, laser pointer dots, or custom uploaded `.cur`/`.ani` files).

---

## 4. Use Cases & Benefits
* **Precision Work:** Graphic designers, developers, and UI/UX QA engineers can map out exact dimensions using rulers before hitting record.
* **Enhanced Tutorials:** The pointer zoom eliminates the need for post-production editing when highlighting specific UI elements, tiny menus, or code snippets in video tutorials.
* **Visual Accessibility:** Custom cursors ensure the audience can easily track the instructor's movements, even on high-resolution displays (4K+).

title: [Feature Request] Detect source framerate upon import
author: cyberspacecat
body:
I would like the app to automatically detect the source framerate when trying to convert a video file to GIF - currently it just defaults to the last manually set framerate, which is not very useful when videos may have a variety of framerates.
This is especially noticeable for animations where a mismatched framerate may cause frames to be dropped and loops to be broken unless the user manually inspects the video details and sets the correct framerate themselves.

title: [Feature Request] Please add Save As with original filename
author: cyberspacecat
body: Forgive me if there is some option I missed, but for the sake of expediency I would like the ability to just keep the same file name as the source file when saving, instead it keeps defaulting simply to "Animation" or "Video" - it's just an annoying extra step that shouldn't be needed as the source file is already there.

title: [Feature Request] Hope to add lossless save function for clipping GIF files
author: Maozai-Teng
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
For some editing functions that are originally GIF files, such as deleting certain frames or just rearranging the order of some frames, when I save them again, the current software re-encodes them, and sometimes it results in the edited file being larger than before.

For example, a 10M GIF file, I deleted the first half of the frames. My expectation was definitely to keep the original quality of the GIF, and then the size would be about 5M. But the current situation is that if I choose to save the best quality, even though my operation was to delete half the frames, the final file I get is likely to be larger than 10M, or even 20M, which completely goes against the user's expectations.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Add a save option: Do not re-encode, just trim or rearrange the frames of the GIF.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
There is an open-source software called gifsicle that solves this problem, and its size is no more than 500KB, which is a great software. Unfortunately, it doesn't have a GUI and can only be operated via the command line, and it cannot display each frame's image, so it cannot be operated simply and conveniently.
If integrating this software/its features into yours, combined with your ability to display each frame, and your great software interface and operation, that would be fantastic~

title: [Feature Request] Recorder Dimension Profiles / Presets for Quick Access
author: eryet
body:
Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.
I frequently need to record at specific pixel dimensions for different purposes (e.g., 1280×720 for documentation, 400×300 for UI demos, 1920×1080 for full-HD captures). Every time I open the recorder, I have to manually type in the width and height values. There's no way to save or quickly switch between these commonly used dimensions, making repetitive recording workflows unnecessarily tedious.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
A Recorder Profile system that lets users save and quickly switch between named dimension presets directly from the recorder toolbar. For example:

- Profile 1 — 1280 × 720 (labeled "HD")
- Profile 2 — 400 × 300 (labeled "UI Demo")
- Profile 3 — 1920 × 1080 (labeled "Full HD")

The profiles could appear as a small dropdown or numbered buttons (Profile 1 / 2 / 3) next to the width/height fields. Clicking a profile instantly applies that dimension to the recorder window. Profiles should be configurable via Options, where users can set the name, width, height, and optionally FPS per profile.

**Additional context**
This pattern is common in other recording/capture tools (e.g., ShareX's region presets, OBS canvas profiles). Even 3 quick-access slots would significantly speed up workflows where you switch between target dimensions often — such as when producing assets for multiple platforms or screen sizes.

title: [Bug] Recorder issue in a dual-monitor setup
author: cinnoga2-ctrl
body: **Describe the bug** In a dual-monitor setup where the right monitor (Monitor 2) is set as the primary display, moving a recorder window using the right arrow key causes it to glitch. Instead of moving further right, the window suddenly jumps to the boundary between Monitor 1 and Monitor 2 and becomes stuck, refusing to move any further to the right. **To Reproduce** Steps to reproduce the behavior: 1. Connect two monitors side-by-side (Monitor 1 on the left, Monitor 2 on the right). 2. Set Monitor 2 (the right one) as the Primary Monitor in display settings. 3. Open a recorder window on Monitor 2. 4. Attempt to move the window to the right using the Right Arrow key. **Expected behavior** See the window jump to the gap between the two monitors and stop responding to further rightward movement. **Screenshots** **Desktop (please complete the following information):** - OS: Windows 11 64bit - Version 2.42.1
